---
title: "Creating A Field"
---

You can create and configure the fields associated with each document class. A field may be applied to several different document classes. When creating a field, you can configure the data type and format of the field. If the Field Suggestions option is enabled, the Fields Suggestions area displays a list of suggested fields for each document class.

Field extraction can be done from text or tabular data present in the documents.

You can also configure the data extraction method for a field as either based on the text layout or based on the image.

To create a field:

1.  On the left navigation pane of the Class page, click the relevant class for which you want to create fields. The right pane displays a list of suggested fields corresponding to the document class selected by you.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)  Note:
- The Fields Suggestions area displays a list of suggested fields for each document class only if the Field Suggestions option is enabled. To enable or disable field suggestions, click the ellipsis button and toggle the Field Suggestions option.
- Click the Refresh icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,) to refresh the list of suggested fields.
- Click Accept All to accept all field suggestions.
- Click Reject All to reject all field suggestions.
- The Class Fields area displays the fields linked to the selected class.
- The Linked Fields area displays the fields linked to the parent class of the selected child class.
2.  On the right pane, click Add Field. The New Field (document class) area is displayed on the right.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  Select the extraction type.
-  Text: Select this option to extract written content such as, titles, names, and other textual data.
-  Table: Select this option to extract tabular data such as inventories or anything that requires a grid layout.
-  Reasoning: Select this option to extract data by visually analysing the document layout. The OCR confidence is not displayed for this type of extraction.
Note: If you select Table as the Extraction Type, you need to define the table columns in which extracted data is displayed. To enter the names of the columns, in the Custom Output Format area, click Add Column.
4.  Enter a name for the field in Field Name.
5.  Enter a description for the field in Field Configuration description.  Note: Avoid entering field configuration descriptions that are too specific when you are extracting data from a variety of documents.
6.  Select the data extraction method for the field you want to create.  Note: The extraction method options are displayed when you select Text as the extraction type.
-  Text: Select this option to use OCR to extract information from visible text and layout. Additionally, you can select either of the following options:
-  Ignore field for extraction: Select this check box if you dont want to extract values for this field.
-  Hide field for Field Review: Select this check box if you want to hide this field during field review.
-  Full File: Select this option for a deeper analysis of the whole document and data reasoning. The OCR confidence is not displayed for full file extraction.
7.  On the left navigation pane, select a document belonging to the class for which you added fields. The document is displayed in the Document Viewer area.
8.  On the right pane, click Extract. The values from all the fields in the document are extracted.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)  Note:
- You can hover on the extracted field names to highlight the position of the keywords on the document.
- Each field extracted value displays the OCR and Extraction percentage.
- The Class Fields area displays the fields linked to the selected class.
- The Linked Fields area displays the fields linked to the parent class of the selected child class.
- To modify the details of the field such as, Field Name, Extraction Type, and the field's description, click the Edit icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,).
- To change the order in which the fields are extracted, you can click the Move up ![](data:image/gif;base64,) or Move down ![](data:image/gif;base64,) arrows.
- To delete a field, click the Delete icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,).
- To navigate to the page from where the field value is extracted, click the Navigate to this field's location icon ![](data:image/gif;base64,).
- To extract all fields, click the ellipsis next to the class name on the right pane, and then click Extract fields in class.
- To remove all extracted field values from the document, click the ellipsis next to the class name on the right pane, and then click Clear all fields.
- To remove all extracted field values from all documents in this class, click the ellipsis next to the class name on the right pane, and then click Clear all fields in class.
- To delete all fields, click the ellipsis next to the class name on the right pane, and then click Delete fields in class. You can choose to delete all fields, including the fields in the parent class or you can detach the fields of the selected clas from the parent class and delete the detached fields only.
- To go back to the form, click the ellipsis next to the class name on the right pane, and then click Navigate to Form.
- To detach a child class from a parent class:
1. Click the ellipsis next to the class name on the right pane, and then click Detach class from parent. A confirmation message is displayed. ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
You can choose to keep or remove its linked fields. Do either of the following:

2. Click Detach and Remove Fields to detach the parent class and remove the linked fields.
Or,

3. Click Detach and Keep Fields to detach the parent class but retain the linked fields.Note: This action cannot be reversed.

The IDP configuration is complete. After IDP configuration is complete, you can create a processing flow for your documents. For more information on how to create a processing flow, see Configuring Document Processing.
